mnemonics: Test components are like a recipe: you need Ingredients (Test Data), Instructions (Test Cases), a Taste Test (Expected Results), and a final Review (Test Report) to ensure it works correctly.
keyPoints: A test component is a set of conditions or variables under which a tester determines whether a system under test satisfies requirements.
Key components include Test Data, Test Cases, Expected Results, and a Test Report.
The purpose is to verify the correctness, completeness, and quality of a software application or data structure.
commonMistakes: Testing with incomplete or unrealistic test data that doesn't cover edge cases.
Not documenting or comparing the actual results against the expected results, leading to false positives.
Focusing only on 'happy path' scenarios and neglecting to test for potential errors or failures.
